Launch Emails Summer '26 Launch

**

- ![checked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    ## Email 1: YOU NEED TO BE GOOD AT 3 THINGS: 
    

Send to: everyone except buyers/alum & the opt out tag 

Send date: Monday, July 29th @ 1 pm EST 

Subj: business for witches (3 step PLAN) 🧹

Alt: ITS HERE!!!! (Time sensitive!) 🥂

Preview text: what, like it’s hard? 💸  
Internal Text: PTB 1 [BOH June ‘26]

  
  

Wanna know something (very comforting) that i’ve realised this year, NAME… ?

  
The more NICHE my clients get, the SIMPLER online business starts looking. 

  

I've worked with the most unique and niche businesses (im actively calling in MORE weird girl clients this year btw 🧚). from interior designers, and publicists, to witches, and sex coaches. I’ve worked with a sudoku expert, a montessori homeschool teacher, every kind of therapist, astrologist, bookkeeper and and other “normal” service providers with refreshing POVs on their industry.

  

after being bts of thousands of businesses at this point, there alllways seems to be a clear common denominator to a successful business.. 

  

(i get asked all the time) so I have a feeling you’re wondering too.. 

  

![](data:image/png;base64,)

  
  

It has NOTHIN’ to do with your sales skills, public speaking ability. Or if you’re blowing up on IG (gasp)  
  

You see, the internet’s been on a mission to convince us that we need to be good at short form, viral, long form, SEO, metrics, running ads, podcasting, brand voice, and on and on…  

  

We’re sold that the “winners” are “consistent” (ew) , and have an elaborate process that “does the work for them” (im tired just typing this).

  

But I’m here to call bluff and say, in my experience,  it ALWAYS comes down to 3 (really simple) things:

  

1. Growing your audience
    

I personally love pinterest for this bc it’s the most low maintenance way to generate leads. But I’ve also used Facebook ads, I have fun occasionally (and accidentally) going viral on IG, and I’ve closed clients in my DMs. I’m not above any of it.

  

2. Writing good emails
    

I’m not going to give you options here(sry). bc ultimately, if you want to sell your services, courses, or consulting, email is KING. and learning how to write rlly fking good emails is something Claude can’t teach you (contrary to popular belief).

  

3. Giving a shit about whatever you do
    

This is the secret sauce, babe.

  

nothing that I teach you will work if you don't have something for it to amplify

  

Back of House easily handles 1 & 2. 

  

We add the pinterest system that grows your audience without posting daily, and the email system that turns those new ppl into clients while you’re doing (literally) anything else.

  

Number three is on you.

  

  
Actually, one of the most dramatic turnaround I've watched happen was a client who came to me with hundreds of thousands of Instagram followers and was somehow still barely making rent 🤔. By the time we wrapped up, she had consistent recurring revenue (to the tune of 60k per month & growing). 

  

Everyone wants to believe that if they JUST had more IF followers all their problems would be solved…. But guess what.. 

  

I didn't change a THING about what she was doing on IG (obviously she had that figured out before we ever started working together) 

  

And I DIDN'T have to worry about #3 .. because what she really gave a shit about (and I mean, obsessed about) was how to build a real badonkadonk from a flat ass🍑

  

My only job was to get her audience onto her email list  and (#2) let her back of house send em really good emails. 

  

and just like that they went from just watching her IG stories, to paying her month after month!

  

Simple as 1,2,3

  

You bring your weird obsession, 

I’ll bring the back of house.

  

[BLURB]

  

---

- ![checked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    Email 2: 
    

Send to: everyone except buyers/alum & the opt out  tag  

Send date: Tuesday, July 30 @ 11 am EST 

Subj: Social Media Divorce [coupon inside]

Preview text: & the perfect mango 🤌

Internal Text: PTB 2 [BOH June ‘26]

  

If you can write hooks in your sleep, and have a hunch which algo update is coming next… 

this is not for you babe 

( feel free to go now <3 )

BUT 

🥭 if you know the perfect time to pick a mango 

[this is for you](http://www.alyssacoleman.ca/boh) 

🥺 if you know just the right thing to say to get a kid to stop having a meltdown 

💅 if you can spot someone's color season from across the room 

this is for you 

🤓 If you know the exact bilirubin level that I should look for on my blood test, a secret Photoshop trick  that turns a basic logo into a luxury one, If you know the Greek word for longing, and why it matters…. 

This is for you.

BC your weird obsessions, unique POVs, secret formulas, and niche interests are what make you MARKETABLE BABE. 

And unfortunately, THESE are the type of people the internet is trying to turn into marketers, and I think that's one of the saddest things happening

WE DON’T NEED ANOTHER MARKETING EXPERT, pls.

Because while they’re trying to go viral, I'm watching the most interesting people in the room start to undervalue their own interests!!?

  
🤔 But what if you DIDN'T have to care about your latest post’s metrics? 

  
🤔 What if you could just be the best at what you’re already best at? 

  
🤔 Because you know your next client is coming from elsewhere

  

The clients who get the most out of [Back of House](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh) are not the ones who know the perfect way to pitch their offer in their bio… 

They’re the ones who are obsessed with what they do. They’re weird. they’re niche. they have a point of view.

[In Back of House, we’ve designed a system FOR creatives.](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh) That handles the lead gen, the nurturing, the converting ... .so you get to divorce yourself from relying on social media (if you want to) and focus on the things you’re OBSESSED with instead.

[blurb] 

---

- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    Email 3: 
    

Send to: everyone except buyers/alum & the opt out tag 

Send date: Thursday, Aug 1  @ 1 pm EST 

Subj: I love old men.

Preview text: Mansplain to me baby!  
Internal Text: PTB 3 [BOH June ‘26]

  

I have a confession, name 

  

i'm a feminist, and love listening to old men 

  

( WEE LIIIISTEN AND WE DOOOON'T JUDGE!!!! )

I hate to admit it, but its true..

  

 i love old men! 

  

Ok ok, it isn't old men themselves (although I really do love david attenborough) ..

  

It's the wise-old-man-ideas that just KEEP being true. 

  

Most of my favourite business books are painfully boring. 

  

I’d re-read How To Win Friends And Influence People 3x before the term “influencer” was even a thing. 

  

And The Seven Habits of Highly Effective People was the catalyst for quitting my “dream job” to work for myself at 22 (i was a highly effective waitress for a while after that lol..)

  

The thing I'm drawn to about these old dudes is that none of them taught HACKS. 

  

they taught human behaviour and [PRINCIPLES](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh) that applied to Henry Ford in 1920, and still made sense when I re-read ‘em in 2020. 

  

They're basically books about...

👴 keeping promises

👴 caring about people

👴 showing up

👴 being useful

  

Simple *rules* of successful, happy, fulfilled humans.

  

The things that have worked across wildly different economic climates, before the internet existed, before email existed

  

before ANY of the things we think now are essential were invented.

  

These foundational rules are the patterns I can spot

  

And Back of House is built on the same boring principles 

  

🔍 People search when they have a problem

💌 ppl buy from people they trust

🥺 ppl forget you if they never hear from you

  

📌 Pinterest gets searched

💌 emails build trust

🥺 good offers solve problems

  

those truths haven't changed in decades

  

In a world of trending audios and internet fast food 

  

[Back of house is your onions, garlic, white wine and butter.](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh)

  

Marketing Mirepoix. 

  

Simple *rules* of successful, happy, fulfilled entrepreneurs.

[one order of reliable & boring pls >>]

  

None of this is new, and I didn't come up with it myself.

  

To put it really simply, I looked back at what has always worked, and I built a system around it (so i could sleep better, automate the paycheck to myself, and get back to things that felt more fulfilling) 

  

That's NOT the most exciting pitch in your inbox today…

  

But I'd bet my highlighted copy of The 7 Habits on it.

  

xo  
  

PS: There's a specific kind of person who prefers reading old books, making things with their hands, and has exactly zero interest in being on the cutting edge of anything. This program was designed for her. [Details here](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh) <<

  

---

- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    Email 4: 
    

Send to: everyone except buyers/alum & the opt out tag 

Send date: Friday, Aug 2 @ 11 am EST

Subj: LAST CALL BABE!

Preview text: art girl (you) 🤝History girl (me)  
Internal Text: PTB 4 [BOH June ‘26]

  

I’ve always wanted to be an art girl, name.

  

The truth is, i’ve spent most of my life surrounding myself with brilliantly creative people, 

  

I was obsessed with them honestly, 

  

the ones who could look at a bolt of old fabric and somehow see that it was gonna *be everywhere* next season. 

  

The one who bought THOSE adidas, then we ALL had those adidas (she was already wearing cooler different shoes by then of course) 

  

I've never BEEN an art girl.  

  

Im just a history girl.

  

I wasn't inventing the thing, i was studying why it… worked 

I was the one who figured out which campus form we needed, so we could borrow the projector and book the seminar room (and who the hell had the key 🗝️).

I was the one drinking wine out of a plastic cup at 3am while they rewrote their concept pitch for the 12th time (and breaking the news that this version was.. unfortunately.. the worst yet lol), and the one who met them the next day for brunch after they finally nailed it.

i was the one who noticed that their latest piece overlapped with the one they made last semester and how it kinda reminded me of the one they showed me from grade 7, and how they kinda maybe had a throughline that might be their entire design perspective? 

It's a love story really 🤎

History girl 🤝art girl 

I've spent a long time making peace with that

And here’s where i've landed .. 

Im never gonna be a futurist - im not the one who’s gonna tell you what’s coming (fk im a little afraid of what's coming, honestly - but that’s a dif email) 

What i CAN do is tell you [what’s always worked](http://www.alyssacoleman.ca/back), what the patterns say and what’s glaringly obvious to everyone else that you’re SO damn good at (and is about to be the next big thing).

[And i get to hand over the system that makes it all work behind the scenes](http://www.alyssacoleman.ca/back) 

  

So that art girls never stop being art girls to become marketers 

  

Here’s how 👇

  

---

- ![unchecked](data:image/png;base64,)
    
    Email 5: 
    

Send to: everyone except buyers/alum & the opt out tag 

Send date: Friday, Aug 2 @ 9 pm EST

Subj: I can't stop thinking about a dead lady

Preview text: the worst sales email i’ve ever written  
Internal Text: PTB 5 [BOH June ‘26]

  

Sooo… I can't stop thinking about a dead lady. 

  

A few weekends ago I walked down an old street in a part of town i’d never been to, omw to an estate sale. An orange cat ran in the front door of the house i was looking for, and was promptly tossed back outside by a well dressed old man.

  

Once i got in I was shocked, the house was so much more beautiful than it looked in the photos, and the things for sale were MUCH better (dream sunday find!).  
  

I made out with 5 heavy lamps, a massive collection of local art she never hung, mixed with some of her own (pretty terrible) amateur paintings. 

  

… and a dresser that she inherited from her parents, (and had refinished in the 70s), with a mystery stain inside one drawer.

  

( for $30!)

  

While one of the women helping with the action helped me carry the old dresser down awkward stairs, I admired the back garden. 

  

She insisted i put the dresser down so she could give me a tour (auction girlies yknow). 

  

We stepped out back and she told me more about the lady who lived here, she was a professor at the university nearby, she lived alone, and bought the house herself in the 60s. We were surrounded by gorgeous tall flowers, and she couldn't believe the thing hadn’t been watered in months, and there wasn’t a single weed. 

  

She added “yeah, she actually became kind of a self-taught expert on indigenous plants” 

  

Before I could reply, my daughter walked out holding something that looked QUITE breakable. And the tour was over

  

..  but i havent stopped thinking about this dead lady. 

  

  
Because you know what, she really wasn’t impressive by internet standards. 

  

The house was small, looked pretty bad in photos, and her stuff was honestly.. Kinda a mess. 

  

🥺 But something in my BONES knew she had a good life. 

  
.. Maybe she didn't ever hang that art, 

.. and her watercolour work didnt improve much from what i can tell…

.. but I bet she was a good professor.

.. and I bet she told her friends what she learned about indigenous plants ..

.. and I bet they all had nice gardens. 

And i bet, by human standards, she enjoyed her life.

  
My new (ghost) mentor wasn’t concerned about impressing the internet, and lately - neither am i. 

I’m not posting my launch #s or monthly revenue, not turning every client win into a sales claim with a heavy disclaimer 

  

I've done that before

  

👀 And it often worked..

💵 People were impressed. 

  

But it wasn’t for me. 

  

And while this is likely the WORST sales email i’ve ever written … 

  

[Since back of house closes in a couple hours](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh) and i haven't mentioned ANYTHING about the ROI of joining, or the impressive success stories, or the value of the (incredible) bonuses that are disappearing tn. (!!)

  

It’s … what was inspiring to ME today.

  

And THAT’s the sales strategy I'm betting on lately. 

  

I'm not here to promise you the perfect internet business

  

[But I can offer a business Back of House, that leaves enough of you left for ALL your side quests](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh)

So you can become the kind of person whose estate sale makes strangers think  
"...she must've had a lovely life"

BLURB

  

PS: if you think studying which flowers you can plant once so your garden keeps getting better every year is cool. You’ll love our prep once, forever philosophy inside back of house. [$500 coupon expires tonight xx](https://alyssacoleman.ca/boh)

**
